title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
Transfer connection, buffered unread octets, deadlines, close ownership, and cancellation atomically to the selected protocol or tunnel.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Upgrade Handshake
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 6742 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.

Inspect the Upgrade Handshake trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Upgrade Handshake.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate connection upgrade, upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Upgrade Handshake, keep Upgrade Handshake connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-transition-rejection-recovery-1cacf6" that uses case 6226 to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Transition Rejection Recovery into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery, keep Transition Rejection Recovery connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.why: Retrieving Transition Rejection Recovery through the concrete confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery, keep Transition Rejection Recovery connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Transition Rejection Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 6750 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Continue HTTP/1.1 safely after a rejected transition only when buffered bytes, framing, peer behavior, and parser state remain unambiguous.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Transition Rejection Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every rejected Upgrade or CONNECT must close the connection even when no optimistic bytes were sent.
